differential_diagnoses: Acute kidney injury (acute tubular necrosis vs. cortical necrosis)
next_steps: Fluid resuscitation and initiation of blood component therapy (packed red blood cells, fresh frozen plasma, and platelets)
Obstetric consultation for assessment of fetal distress and emergency delivery if indicated
Urgent transfer to a tertiary facility with hemodialysis capabilities
Serial monitoring of coagulation profile, renal function, and hemoglobin levels
Snake identification verification by an expert

differential_diagnoses: Echis ocellatus envenomation-induced venom-induced consumption coagulopathy (VICC)
Placental abruption secondary to systemic envenomation
Acute kidney injury (acute tubular necrosis and/or cortical necrosis)
Compartment syndrome of the lower extremity
next_steps: Immediate removal of the tourniquet with continuous hemodynamic monitoring
Aggressive fluid resuscitation and administration of blood products (packed red blood cells, fresh frozen plasma, and platelets)
Urgent obstetric consultation for fetal distress and management of placental abruption/hemorrhage
Placement of a Foley catheter for precise monitoring of urine output
Urgent transfer to a tertiary facility with hemodialysis capabilities
Monitoring for compartment syndrome and evaluation for potential fasciotomy
Tetanus prophylaxis and initiation of antibiotics
Serial monitoring of coagulation parameters and renal function
